title: sambucus racemosa var. pendula
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. pendula, also known as the dwarf black elderberry or weeping elder, is a deciduous shrub that is native to North America and is a variety of the American elderberry. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the presence of flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa var. pendula include relief from cold and flu symptoms, as well as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Sambucus racemosa var. pendula can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
